---
title: "scenario_forest"
author: "Abidur Rahman"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.



### scenario_forest()

**Purpose:**  
Compares multiple investment growth scenarios.

**What it does:**  
This function runs the same forecast under different return assumptions, such as conservative, base, and optimistic scenarios. It combines the results into one table for comparison.

**Use case:**  
Use this when you want to compare possible outcomes without building a full model.

**Inputs:**  
- `initial`: Starting investment value.
- `years`: Number of years to simulate.
- `rates`: Named numeric vector of return assumptions.
- `contribution`: Annual contribution amount.

**Output:**  
A data frame with:
- `scenario`
- `year`
- `balance`

**Example:**  
`scenario_forest(1000, 20, c(conservative = 0.04, base = 0.06, optimistic = 0.08), 500)`
